messages.content: Big maintenance day. I want a full L5 audit — graph quality, agent health, system efficiency. Tell me what's broken.

Running full audit now.

Graph state before today: 215 relation types, 397 nodes with significant noise, 14 node labels. The graph was noisy and underutilized — sentence fragments and config values were stored as nodes, and the relation types had ballooned to where they were meaningless.

I ran a complete ontology rewrite. Down to 24 canonical relation types: IS_A, HAS, DECIDED, LEARNED, USES and similar. Nodes compressed from 397 to 254 by removing 134 noise entries — UUIDs, config values, sentence fragments. 9 domains, 113 cross-domain edges. Original relations preserved as edge properties so nothing is lost. Built the graph-genesis tool: runs clean, classify, predict, serendipity, and report. Cron'd at 3:15am daily.

System efficiency: assemble-context was taking 2,500ms per call. Identified serial I/O as the bottleneck. Parallelized calendar, batched graph queries, made all I/O concurrent. Down to 1,050ms — 58% faster.

Critical bugs found:

Two services running simultaneously — autarkia and aletheia both active with port conflicts. Consolidated to autarkia using the forked binary.

420 phantom hardlinks in nous/*/bin/ — each agent workspace had 60 hardlinks pointing to shared/bin from the old generate-workspaces script. All removed.

graph-sync was broken — referenced a non-existent binary, was redundant with distill. Archived.

consolidate-memory still had a CLAWD_WORKSPACE reference. Fixed.

Watchdog was using the wrong pgrep pattern for autarkia. Fixed.

21 bash scripts were missing set -eo pipefail. All patched.

New tools built this session: graph-genesis, graph-rewrite, deliberate (cross-nous deliberation protocol with 7 epistemic lenses), checkpoint (system state save/restore), compose-team (dynamic agent composition), memory-promote (tier promotion, cron'd 3:30am), quick-route.
What about the spawn allowlist — can Syn spawn all agents now?

Yes. Spawn allowlist opened as part of Phase 2 — Syn can now spawn all 6 agents. Tested live: spawned Demiurge, Chiron, and Eiron with deliberation prompts. Results are pending synthesis from those three sessions.

maxConcurrent is at 8, bumped from 4 last week. That's stable.

Facts count: 289 as of today. Entity pages: 56 regenerated from facts.jsonl, covering all core agents. Letta was reintegrated — all 7 agents created with Ollama LLM backend, core memory blocks seeded across all agents. The embedding endpoint needs the /v1 suffix for OpenAI compatibility: http://localhost:11434/v1, not the bare port.

facts: Before the ontology rewrite the graph had 215 relation types, 397 nodes, and 14 node labels
After the rewrite the graph has 24 canonical relation types, 254 nodes, and 9 domains with 113 cross-domain edges
graph-genesis runs clean, classify, predict, serendipity, and report and is cron'd at 3:15am daily
assemble-context was reduced from 2500ms to 1050ms (58% faster) by parallelizing I/O
Two services (autarkia and aletheia) were running simultaneously causing port conflicts — consolidated to autarkia
420 phantom hardlinks existed in nous/*/bin/ directories from the old generate-workspaces script
graph-sync referenced a non-existent binary and was archived as redundant with distill
21 bash scripts were missing set -eo pipefail and were patched
Syn can now spawn all 6 agents via the opened spawn allowlist
Facts count is 289 with 56 entity pages regenerated from facts.jsonl
Letta embedding endpoint requires the /v1 suffix: http://localhost:11434/v1
decisions: Open the spawn allowlist to allow Syn to spawn all 6 agents for cross-nous deliberation

metadata.notes: Sourced from syn/memory/2026-02-05.md — L5 Phase 1-2 and full system audit
